summary: Soundboard recording patched to fix a gap, delivering the historic first Scarlet > Fire medley and standout renditions of Sugaree, Peggy‑O, Cassidy and Not Fade Away.
review: The patched soundboard capture fixes a notorious edit and offers generally clear audio, though a few reviewers note the transition could be smoother. Musically this show is celebrated for the first-ever Scarlet > Fire medley and strong performances of fan favorites like Sugaree, Peggy‑O and Not Fade Away, with Jerry’s guitar and vocals praised as back on track.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews are 5‑star, highlighting both a historically significant performance and a solid soundboard source (with only minor transition complaints).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Patch fixes the major gap in the original; audio is generally clear, but some listeners wish the patched transition sounded more seamless.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Highly praised for its historic first Scarlet > Fire medley and a smooth, energetic flow of classic Dead favorites; the patched edit improves continuity.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar work is described as back on track with an ‘oh‑so good’ vocal performance throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for helping get Jerry back on track, indicating solid supportive playing.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Godchaux’s keyboards and Donna Jean’s vocals are said to be on top of their game.
